text.title: Code Review Process Core Rules
text.summary: 5 hard rules for a working code-review process.

rule.statement: Repo has a PR template enforcing 'what + why + tests + risks' sections.

rationale.source: GitHub docs — PR templates; Google eng practices

rule.rationale: Templates set author expectations; missing sections create rework loops.

rule.statement: Default branch requires: ≥1 human reviewer, CI green, no force-push.
rule.rationale: Without protection any review process is advisory.

rule.statement: Four scenario templates (bug fix / new design / security / perf) are published; reviewer picks one before the deep review.
rule.rationale: Different scenario shapes need different checklists; one template doesn't fit all.

rule.statement: Three metrics tracked: time-to-first-review, cycle time, rework rate. Dashboard refreshed weekly.
rule.rationale: Untracked process drifts; trackable metrics drive deliberate change.

rule.statement: Bundle is reviewed every 90 days against current platform features (e.g. new GitHub merge queue).
rule.rationale: Platform capabilities evolve; stale config misses leverage.
